title: Tailor resume bullet for a job description
description: Customize a resume achievement to match job requirements.

title: Cover letter for a specific role
description: Write a compelling cover letter tailored to the job posting.

title: Professional bio
description: Create a compelling professional biography for bios and profiles.

title: Portfolio project description
description: Write a compelling description of a project for your portfolio.

title: Behavioral interview answer using STAR
description: Structure a compelling behavioral interview answer using the STAR method.

title: Questions to ask at the end of an interview
description: Generate thoughtful questions to ask interviewers that show genuine interest.

title: Explain an employment gap
description: Address employment gaps in interviews with honesty and confidence.

title: Asking for an internal referral
description: Ask someone you know for a referral to a position.

title: Request an informational interview
description: Request a conversation with someone in your target role or company.

title: Negotiate a job offer via email
description: Professionally negotiate compensation and benefits in a job offer.

title: Counter a lowball offer
description: Professionally counter an unacceptably low job offer.

title: Script for asking about salary range
description: Confidently ask about compensation during early conversations.

title: Introduce yourself in a new role
description: Send an intro message to your new team/department.

title: Ask for feedback on your work
description: Request constructive feedback from manager or colleague in a growth-oriented way.

title: Request a mentor
description: Respectfully ask someone to mentor you professionally.

title: LinkedIn post about a career win
description: Share a professional achievement on LinkedIn in an authentic way.

title: Resign professionally
description: Write a resignation letter that maintains relationships and reputation.

title: Respond to a rejection professionally
description: Reply to a job rejection in a way that keeps the door open.

title: 30-60-90 day plan for a new role
description: Create a structured plan for your first 90 days in a new position.
body:
Create a 30-60-90 day plan for my new {{job_title}} role at {{company_name}}.

Role context: {{role_overview}}
Key priorities: {{main_responsibilities}}
Stakeholders: {{important_people}}
Success metrics: {{how_you'll_measure_success}}

Structure it as:
- 30 Days: Learn (people, processes, codebase/product)
- 60 Days: Contribute (first real projects/wins)
- 90 Days: Impact (measurable results and credibility)

Include:
- Key meetings and learning activities
- Early wins to build momentum
- Metrics to track progress
- Questions to ask and answer

title: Asking for a raise or promotion
description: Request a raise or promotion with data-backed justification.

title: Cold outreach email
description: Write a concise, direct cold email that gets replies.
body:
Write a cold outreach email to a {{recipient_role}} at {{company}}.

Context:
- What I offer: {{your_offering}}
- Their likely pain point: {{pain_point}}
- What I want them to do: {{call_to_action}}

Requirements:
- Subject line under 8 words
- Email body under 120 words
- No buzzwords or filler phrases
- End with one clear question, not a statement
- Tone: direct and respectful, not salesy

Write the subject line first, then the email body.

title: Professional feedback email
description: Send constructive feedback that is specific, kind, and leads to action.
body:
Help me write a professional email to {{recipient_name}} about {{topic}}.

My feedback: {{your_feedback}}
What I want to happen: {{desired_outcome}}

Make it:
- Constructive, not critical
- Specific, not vague
- Under 150 words
- Ending with a clear next step
- Tone: collaborative and respectful
- Show you care about their growth

title: Thank someone for their help
description: Express genuine gratitude in a professional way.

title: Apology email for a mistake
description: Write a sincere apology that takes responsibility and commits to change.

title: Saying no professionally
description: Decline a request while maintaining good relationships.
body:
Help me say no professionally to {{recipient_name}}'s request for {{what_they_asked}}.

Why you're declining: {{reason_for_declining}}
Alternatives you can offer: {{alternatives}}
How to maintain relationship: {{way_forward}}

Make the response:
- Clear and direct (not wishy-washy)
- Appreciative of the ask
- Explain briefly why you can't (no long excuses)
- Offer alternatives if possible
- Leave door open for future
- Keep it under 100 words
- Suggest another way forward

title: Meeting follow-up with action items
description: Send a clear follow-up that captures decisions and keeps everyone accountable.

title: Project status update
description: Send a clear status update that shows progress and addresses blockers.

title: Difficult conversation script
description: Prepare for a challenging conversation with a structured script.

title: Delegating a task clearly
description: Delegate work effectively with clear expectations and support.

title: Writing a performance review
description: Write a constructive performance review that inspires growth.

title: Requesting a testimonial or review
description: Ask for a professional testimonial or recommendation.

title: Announcing a policy change
description: Communicate a new or changed policy clearly and persuasively.

title: Out of office message
description: Create an informative out-of-office auto-reply.

title: Negotiating a deadline extension
description: Ask for more time on a project or deliverable professionally.

title: Escalating a complaint professionally
description: Escalate an issue when normal channels haven't resolved it.

title: Introducing two people via email
description: Make a warm introduction that adds value for both parties.

title: Following up after no response
description: Send a polite but persistent follow-up without being annoying.

title: Debug this error message
description: Get help understanding and fixing an error.

title: Code review feedback
description: Provide constructive code review comments.

title: Explain code to a non-technical person
description: Break down technical concepts in plain language.
body:
Help me explain {{technical_concept}} to {{audience}} in simple terms.

What it does: {{what_it_accomplishes}}
Why it matters: {{business_impact}}
Who/what it affects: {{scope}}
Analogy or comparison: {{good_analogy}}

Make the explanation:
- No jargon or define terms used
- Use analogies and real-world examples
- Focus on "what" and "why", not "how"
- Keep it under 3 minutes to read
- Use short sentences
- Add a helpful analogy
- Ask if they have questions
- Be genuinely helpful, not condescending

title: Write unit tests
description: Generate unit tests for a function or module.

title: Refactor code for readability
description: Improve code clarity and maintainability.

title: Write a SQL query
description: Generate a SQL query to solve a database problem.

title: Write a bash or Python script
description: Generate an automated script to solve a problem.

title: Optimize code for performance
description: Improve code speed and efficiency.

title: Write API documentation
description: Create clear documentation for an API endpoint.

title: Security review of code
description: Identify and fix security vulnerabilities.

title: Write a regex pattern
description: Create a regular expression for pattern matching.

title: Explain a technical concept simply
description: Break down a complex technical topic.

title: Write a git commit message
description: Craft a clear, useful commit message.

title: Database schema design
description: Design a database schema for your data model.

title: Explain a system architecture decision
description: Justify architectural choices and patterns.

title: Write error handling for a function
description: Add comprehensive error handling to code.

title: Plan an API integration
description: Design the integration of an external API.

title: Code walkthrough for onboarding
description: Guide a new team member through the codebase.

title: Summarize a document for a specific audience
description: Create a tailored summary for different audiences.

title: Compare two options with a framework
description: Evaluate options against criteria using a structured framework.

title: Pros and cons breakdown
description: Create a balanced list of advantages and disadvantages.
body:
Help me create a pros and cons breakdown for {{topic}}.

Context: {{situation_or_proposal}}
Stakeholders: {{who_is_affected}}
Time frame: {{relevant_timeframe}}
Decision being made: {{what_decision}}

Provide:
1. Pros (with impact/importance rating)
2. Cons (with impact/importance rating)
3. Neutral considerations
4. Long-term implications
5. Short-term impacts
6. Risk factors
7. Weighted analysis
8. Recommendation based on analysis

title: First principles breakdown
description: Deconstruct a complex problem to fundamentals.
body:
Help me break down {{problem_or_topic}} using first principles.

What I know: {{existing_knowledge}}
What I want to understand: {{learning_goal}}
Assumptions: {{current_beliefs}}

Provide:
1. Core principles involved
2. Fundamental truths (challenge assumptions)
3. How the basics work
4. How they combine to create the system
5. Analogies to similar systems
6. What's unique about this case
7. Implications of the fundamental understanding
8. Application to the problem

title: Find weaknesses in an argument
description: Identify logical fallacies and unsupported claims.
body:
Help me find weaknesses in this argument.

The argument: {{argument_to_analyze}}
Who's making it: {{source}}
Context: {{situational_context}}
Stakeholder interest: {{who_benefits}}

Provide:
1. Logical fallacies present
2. Unsupported claims
3. Missing evidence
4. Alternative explanations
5. Assumptions being made
6. What would strengthen the argument
7. Counterarguments
8. How credible is this argument overall

title: Extract action items from meeting notes
description: Organize meeting notes into clear action items.
body:
Help me extract action items from these meeting notes.

Meeting: {{meeting_name}}
Date: {{meeting_date}}
Attendees: {{who_attended}}
Notes: {{meeting_notes_or_transcript}}

Provide:
1. Organized action items with:
   - What needs to be done
   - Who's responsible
   - When it's due
   - Priority level
2. Decisions made
3. Key discussion points
4. Follow-up items
5. Risks or concerns raised
6. Next meeting agenda items

title: Pressure test a business idea
description: Critically evaluate a business concept.

title: Identify risks in a plan
description: Find potential problems and mitigation strategies.

title: SWOT analysis
description: Analyze strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, threats.
body:
Create a SWOT analysis for {{subject_of_analysis}}.

Context: {{current_situation}}
Time frame: {{analysis_period}}
Comparators: {{what_youre_comparing_against}}
Stakeholders: {{who_is_affected}}

Provide:
1. Strengths (internal, controllable)
2. Weaknesses (internal, controllable)
3. Opportunities (external, could exploit)
4. Threats (external, beyond control)
5. Strategic implications
6. Priority action items
7. Resource allocation recommendations
8. How to leverage strengths against threats

title: Root cause analysis
description: Find the underlying cause of a problem.
body:
Help me find the root cause of {{problem}}.

What happened: {{problem_statement}}
When it started: {{timeline}}
Who/what is affected: {{scope}}
Symptoms: {{observable_symptoms}}
What has been tried: {{attempted_solutions}}

Use the "5 Whys" to:
1. Define the problem clearly
2. Ask why (level 1)
3. Ask why about the answer (level 2)
4. Continue until you find the root cause
5. Verify the root cause
6. Develop prevention strategies
7. Create action plan

Provide:
- Symptom vs. root cause
- Unlikely causes to rule out
- Most probable root cause
- Prevention for future

title: Predict second-order effects
description: Think through indirect consequences of actions.

title: Devil's advocate on a decision
description: Challenge a decision to test its strength.
body:
Help me play devil's advocate on {{decision}}.

The decision: {{what_decision_was_made}}
Reasoning: {{why_this_decision}}
Alternatives considered: {{other_options}}
Who decided: {{decision_makers}}

Provide:
1. Strong counterarguments
2. Hidden assumptions being made
3. What could go wrong
4. Overlooked alternatives
5. Uncomfortable truths
6. Questions that should be answered
7. Conditions where this decision fails
8. Better questions to ask

Goal: Strengthen the decision or uncover flaws

title: Market research and competitive analysis
description: Research market size, trends, and competitors.
body:
Help me research {{market_or_segment}}.

Product/service: {{what_youre_selling}}
Target market: {{who_is_your_customer}}
Geography: {{which_markets}}
Competitors: {{known_competitors}}
Research goals: {{what_you_need_to_know}}

Provide:
1. Market size (total addressable market)
2. Growth rate and trends
3. Customer segments
4. Competitive landscape
5. Key competitors (strengths/weaknesses)
6. Market gaps/opportunities
7. Barriers to entry
8. Data sources and confidence levels
9. Recommendations

Include sources and confidence levels.

title: Scenario planning exercise
description: Prepare for multiple possible futures.
body:
Help me create scenarios for {{situation}}.

Context: {{current_state}}
Time horizon: {{how_far_ahead}}
Key uncertainties: {{what_could_change}}
Decision to prepare for: {{what_decision}}

Create:
1. Best case scenario
   - What goes right
   - Probability estimate
   - Strategy for this case

2. Base case scenario (most likely)
   - Current trends continue
   - Key indicators
   - Strategy for this case

3. Worst case scenario
   - What goes wrong
   - Early warning signs
   - Contingency plan

4. Wildcard scenario (unexpected)
   - Something surprising happens
   - Adaptive strategies

Provide: Early indicators to watch for each

title: Define a problem precisely
description: Clearly articulate the problem you're solving.
body:
Help me define {{problem_area}} precisely.

Observation: {{what_you_noticed}}
Who is affected: {{stakeholders}}
How it matters: {{impact_or_urgency}}
What you think the problem is: {{current_hypothesis}}

Provide:
1. Problem statement (one clear sentence)
2. Problem scope (how big is it)
3. Who is affected and how
4. Root cause hypothesis
5. Current state vs desired state
6. Success criteria (how you'd know it's solved)
7. Constraints on the solution
8. Questions that need answering

Good problems are:
- Specific, not vague
- Important to solve
- Measurable
- Clear about impact

title: Weighted decision matrix
description: Use a scoring system to compare options.
body:
Help me create a weighted decision matrix for {{decision}}.

Options: {{options_to_compare}}
Decision context: {{why_this_matters}}
Criteria to consider: {{decision_factors}}
Constraints: {{limitations}}

Provide:
1. Criteria (what matters)
2. Weight for each criterion (importance)
3. Score each option (0-10)
4. Calculate weighted scores
5. Sensitivity analysis (what if weights change)
6. Recommendation
7. Risks of the chosen option
8. How to validate the choice

Include: Explanation of scores and weights

title: Surface insights from data
description: Find meaningful patterns and conclusions.
body:
Help me analyze {{data_type}} and find insights.

Data: {{your_data_or_dataset}}
Context: {{what_the_data_represents}}
Time period: {{data_timeframe}}
Metrics available: {{what_you_can_measure}}
Goal: {{what_you_want_to_learn}}

Provide:
1. Key statistics (min, max, average, trends)
2. Notable patterns or anomalies
3. Segments (different groups in data)
4. Correlations and relationships
5. Trends over time
6. Outliers and unusual cases
7. Business implications
8. Recommended actions
9. Further analysis needed

Focus on: Actionable insights, not just numbers

title: Product launch announcement
description: Write a compelling product launch announcement.

title: Ad copy for a product
description: Create compelling advertising copy.
body:
Help me write {{ad_type}} ad copy for {{product_name}}.

Product: {{what_it_is}}
Target audience: {{who_you_want_to_reach}}
Main benefit: {{primary_value}}
Unique angle: {{what_makes_it_different}}
Call to action: {{what_you_want_them_to_do}}
Word/character limit: {{length_constraint}}

Include:
1. Attention-grabbing headline
2. Problem/pain point
3. Your solution
4. Key benefit
5. Proof/credibility (if available)
6. Call to action
7. Sense of urgency (if appropriate)

Make it:
- Specific, not generic
- Focused on benefit, not features
- Persuasive but honest
- Easy to understand
- Memorable

title: SEO blog post outline
description: Create an outline for a search-optimized blog post.
body:
Help me outline a blog post on {{topic}}.

Main keyword: {{primary_keyword}}
Target audience: {{reader_profile}}
Post goal: {{what_you_want_readers_to_do}}
Searcher intent: {{why_someone_would_search_this}}
Competitor posts: {{existing_content_on_topic}}

Provide:
1. SEO-friendly title (under 60 characters)
2. Meta description
3. Introduction hook
4. Main sections (H2 headers) with:
   - Subpoints (H3 headers)
   - Key takeaways
5. FAQ section
6. Conclusion with CTA
7. Internal/external link opportunities
8. Target word count

Optimize for:
- Search intent
- Readability
- Keyword placement
- User engagement

title: Landing page hero section
description: Write the headline and subheading for a landing page.
body:
Help me write the hero section for {{page_purpose}}.

Product/service: {{what_youre_promoting}}
Target audience: {{who_this_is_for}}
Main benefit: {{value_proposition}}
Unique angle: {{what_makes_it_special}}
CTA: {{what_you_want_them_to_do}}

Provide:
1. Headline (main benefit in 8-12 words)
2. Subheading (elaboration, 15-20 words)
3. Hero CTA button text
4. Supporting visual description
5. Why these words work

Make it:
- Clear (immediately understandable)
- Specific (not generic)
- Benefit-focused (not feature-focused)
- Compelling (creates interest)
- Action-oriented

title: Customer case study
description: Write a customer success case study.

title: Newsletter intro section
description: Write an engaging newsletter opening.
body:
Help me write the intro section for my newsletter.

Issue topic: {{main_theme}}
Target audience: {{newsletter_audience}}
Tone: {{desired_tone}}
Main message: {{key_point}}
Optional hook: {{story_or_stat}}

Provide:
1. Opening line (attention-grabber)
2. Why this topic matters
3. What they'll learn/get
4. Brief teaser of content
5. Optional P.S. or personal note

Make it:
- Personal and genuine
- Relevant to audience
- Enticing (makes them want to read)
- Scannable
- Under 100 words

title: Five headline variations
description: Generate multiple headline options.
body:
Help me create 5 headline variations for {{content_type}}.

Content: {{what_its_for}}
Target audience: {{who_youre_reaching}}
Primary benefit: {{main_value}}
Unique angle: {{what_stands_out}}
Style preference: {{tone_or_style}}

Create 5 headlines using different approaches:
1. Benefit-driven (what they gain)
2. Problem-focused (what they avoid)
3. Curiosity/intrigue (unexpected angle)
4. Number-based (specific metric)
5. Power word (emotional impact)

For each:
- Test with different audiences if possible
- Consider keyword optimization if for SEO
- Check character count for platform
- Ensure clarity (no clickbait)

Provide: Why each works and when to use

title: Write a press release
description: Create a professional press release.
body:
Help me write a press release about {{announcement}}.

Announcement: {{what_youre_announcing}}
Who is involved: {{companies_or_people}}
Why it matters: {{significance}}
When: {{date_or_timeline}}
Relevant details: {{key_facts}}
Quotes available: {{person_to_quote}}

Structure:
1. Headline (news-style, benefit-focused)
2. Subheading (elaboration)
3. Dateline (City, Date)
4. Opening paragraph (who, what, when, where, why)
5. Body paragraphs (details, significance)
6. Quote from key person
7. Information about companies/people involved
8. Contact information

Make it:
- News-worthy and factual
- Professional tone
- Clear value to readers
- Under 400 words
- Include one quote minimum

title: Brand voice and tone guide
description: Define your brand's communication style.
body:
Help me create a brand voice and tone guide for {{company_name}}.

Brand: {{brand_description}}
Audience: {{target_customer_profile}}
Industry: {{market_context}}
Competitors: {{competitive_positioning}}

Define:
1. Brand personality (3-5 traits)
   - Give examples of how each shows
2. Voice (consistent way of speaking)
   - Vocabulary and phrases
   - Grammar choices
   - Do's and don'ts
3. Tone variations (how it changes by context)
   - Friendly vs professional
   - Casual vs formal
   - Serious vs playful
4. Example phrases showing voice
5. What to avoid
6. How to write for different channels

Provide: Examples of good and bad communication

title: Repurpose content across formats
description: Turn one piece of content into multiple formats.
body:
Help me repurpose {{original_format}} into multiple formats.

Original content: {{original_piece}}
Original audience: {{who_it_was_for}}
Core message: {{main_idea}}
Key points: {{important_takeaways}}

Create versions for:
1. Short-form social (tweets, LinkedIn)
2. Infographic or visual
3. Email/newsletter excerpt
4. Podcast/audio script
5. Video script outline
6. SlideShare presentation
7. Quote graphics
8. Summary/cheat sheet

For each:
- Adapt to platform requirements
- Match audience expectations
- Keep core message
- Optimize for platform engagement

Include: Distribution timeline

title: Social posts from blog content
description: Create social media posts from a blog article.
body:
Help me create social media posts for my {{blog_title}} article.

Blog article: {{article_link_or_content}}
Main topic: {{article_topic}}
Key takeaway: {{most_important_point}}
Target audience: {{social_audience}}
Platforms: {{where_youll_post}}

Create posts for:
1. LinkedIn (professional angle)
2. Twitter/X (punchy, shareable)
3. Instagram (visual angle)
4. Facebook (community engagement)
5. TikTok (trending audio, hook)

For each:
- Optimize for platform
- Include relevant hashtags
- Add call-to-action
- Vary the angle/framing
- Include emoji where appropriate
- Link to article or resource

Provide: Posting suggestions and timing

title: YouTube video description
description: Write a description that drives views and clicks.
body:
Help me write a YouTube description for {{video_title}}.

Video: {{what_the_video_is}}
Topic: {{main_subject}}
Target viewer: {{who_should_watch}}
Key benefit: {{what_they_learn}}
Video length: {{duration}}
Playlist/series: {{if_part_of_series}}

Include:
1. Hook (first 2-3 lines visible without expanding)
   - What the video is about
   - Why they should watch
2. Timestamp markers (if long video)
3. Links:
   - Your website
   - Related videos
   - Social media
   - Products mentioned
4. Call-to-action (like, subscribe, comment)
5. Hashtags (3-5 relevant)
6. Keywords for SEO

Make it:
- Scannable (short paragraphs)
- Link-rich (where relevant)
- Action-oriented (clear CTA)

title: Email subject line split test
description: Create subject line variations to test.
body:
Help me create subject line variations for {{email_type}}.

Email purpose: {{what_youre_promoting}}
Target audience: {{who_is_getting_it}}
Key benefit: {{main_value}}
Urgency level: {{how_time_sensitive}}

Create 5 variations testing different approaches:
1. Curiosity (intriguing, makes them open)
2. Benefit-focused (clear value)
3. Urgency/FOMO (limited time or exclusive)
4. Personalization (name or preference)
5. Question (engages reader)

For each:
- Keep under 50 characters (mobile)
- Avoid spam words
- Make first 3 words compelling
- Test A/B with similar audiences
- Track open rate as metric

Provide: Recommendation on which to test first

title: Content calendar planning
description: Plan your content across channels and platforms.
body:
Help me plan a content calendar for {{timeframe}}.

Business: {{your_company}}
Goals: {{content_goals}}
Audience: {{target_audience}}
Channels: {{where_you_publish}}
Team capacity: {{how_much_you_can_produce}}

Create:
1. Content themes by month
2. Weekly posting schedule
3. Content types per channel:
   - Blog posts
   - Social media posts
   - Email newsletters
   - Videos/podcasts
4. Posting frequency for each channel
5. Promotion strategy
6. Metrics to track
7. Content production timeline
8. Responsible parties

Include:
- Important dates/events
- Product launches
- Industry holidays/awareness months
- Campaign calendar

title: Product positioning statement
description: Craft a clear market positioning.
body:
Help me write a positioning statement for {{product_name}}.

Product: {{what_it_is}}
Target market: {{primary_customer}}
Key benefit: {{main_value}}
Differentiator: {{what_makes_unique}}
Competitors: {{who_you_compete_with}}

Structure:
"For [target customer] who [customer need], {{product_name}} is a [category] that [key benefit]. Unlike [competitor], we [unique value]."

Provide:
1. Clear positioning statement
2. Supporting proof points
3. Brand messaging architecture
4. How to communicate each part
5. Visual/verbal elements
6. How it differs from competitors

Make sure:
- Clear and specific
- Differentiating (not generic)
- Benefit-focused
- Memorable
- Credible

title: Value proposition canvas
description: Map customer jobs, pains, and gains.
body:
Help me complete a value proposition canvas for {{product_name}}.

Product: {{what_you_offer}}
Customer segment: {{primary_customer}}

Customer Profile:
1. Jobs to be done
   - Functional jobs (what they want to accomplish)
   - Emotional jobs (how they want to feel)
   - Social jobs (how they want to be seen)
2. Pains
   - Major pain points they face
   - Severity and frequency
3. Gains
   - Desired outcomes
   - Success measures

Value Map:
1. Products/services offered
2. Pain relievers
   - How you reduce pain
3. Gain creators
   - How you create gains

Provide: Gap analysis and recommendations

title: Product or feature launch checklist
description: Comprehensive checklist for launching.
body:
Help me create a launch checklist for {{product_or_feature}}.

Product: {{what_youre_launching}}
Target audience: {{primary_users}}
Launch date: {{when}}
Team: {{who_is_involved}}
Resources: {{budget_and_constraints}}

Create checklist for:
1. Pre-launch (2-4 weeks before)
   - Product readiness
   - Marketing collateral
   - Support preparation
   - Team alignment

2. Launch day
   - Go-live checklist
   - Monitoring
   - Communications

3. Post-launch (first week)
   - Customer support
   - Issue tracking
   - Metrics tracking
   - User feedback collection

4. Follow-up (first month)
   - Performance analysis
   - Refinements
   - Next features

For each: Owner, deadline, status

title: Sales objection handling guide
description: Prepare responses to common sales objections.
body:
Help me create an objection handling guide for {{product_or_service}}.

Product: {{what_youre_selling}}
Target customer: {{who_buys}}
Price point: {{cost}}
Common objections: {{what_people_say}}

For each common objection:
1. Acknowledge and validate
2. Clarify what they mean
3. Provide perspective
4. Address underlying concern
5. Present your solution
6. Move forward

Common objections to address:
- "It's too expensive"
- "I need to think about it"
- "I don't have time"
- "We use something else"
- "I'm not sure if it will work"
- "Your competitor is cheaper"

For each:
- Root cause of objection
- Best response (empathetic, not defensive)
- Supporting evidence/examples
- How to close after response

title: Trend analysis and implications
description: Analyze trends and their strategic implications.
body:
Help me analyze {{trend}} and its implications.

Trend: {{what_is_trending}}
Industry: {{your_industry}}
Time horizon: {{trend_timeframe}}
Evidence: {{data_points_or_signals}}
Who is affected: {{stakeholders}}

Analyze:
1. Trend confirmation
   - Is this real or hype?
   - Data supporting it
2. Trajectory
   - Will it accelerate or fade?
   - Timeline
3. Drivers
   - What's causing the trend?
4. Implications for your business
   - Opportunities
   - Threats
   - Required changes
5. Response strategies
   - How to capitalize
   - How to protect against threats
6. Early indicators to watch

Provide: Confidence level and key uncertainties

title: Lessons learned retrospective
description: Capture learnings from a project or period.
body:
Help me run a retrospective for {{project_or_period}}.

Project: {{what_you_completed}}
Timeline: {{when_it_happened}}
Team: {{who_was_involved}}
Outcome: {{results}}

Structure:
1. What went well?
   - Successes to celebrate
   - What enabled them

2. What didn't go well?
   - Challenges faced
   - Root causes

3. What did we learn?
   - Insights gained
   - New knowledge

4. What will we do differently?
   - Specific improvements
   - Who owns them
   - By when

5. What should we keep doing?
   - Practices that worked
   - How to reinforce

Provide:
- Action items for next time
- Celebration of wins
- Psychological safety in feedback
